title: QFINHUB Reply Accelerator Prospects — Smaller, Higher-Reply-Likelihood Targets
strategy: Target smaller sites where a real person checks the inbox. Prioritize reply likelihood over domain authority.

prospects.name: Retire by 40
prospects.reply_likelihood: HIGH — Solo blogger, active community, small site. Owner personally answers emails.
prospects.widget_fit: FI-focused readers make big housing decisions. Mortgage affordability widget helps evaluate the biggest expense on the FI path.
prospects.pitch_angle: FI angle: 'Your readers are optimizing every dollar — knowing exactly how much house they can afford is critical to staying on track for early retirement.'

prospects.name: Frugalwoods
prospects.reply_likelihood: HIGH — Personal blog, actively maintained, reader-focused. Owner replies to emails.
prospects.widget_fit: Frugality + housing = natural fit. Readers need to know budget before buying. Free widget aligned with frugal values.
prospects.pitch_angle: Frugality angle: 'Your readers are intentional spenders — our free widget helps them determine the right home budget without overspending.'

prospects.name: Budget Simple Life
prospects.reply_likelihood: HIGH — Small personal blog, debt-free/minimalist focus. Direct Gmail contact.
prospects.widget_fit: Debt-free living audience needs mortgage affordability tools that don't push overspending.
prospects.pitch_angle: Minimalist finance: 'Your readers want simplicity — this widget gives them a clear, honest answer about what they can afford, no upsells.'

prospects.name: My Money Wizard
prospects.reply_likelihood: HIGH — Mid-size personal finance blog, active author. Personal brand.
prospects.widget_fit: Millennial finance audience buying first homes. Affordability widget directly relevant.
prospects.pitch_angle: Millennial homebuyer angle: 'Most of your readers are navigating their first home purchase — this widget gives them instant clarity on budget.'

prospects.name: Can I Retire Yet
prospects.reply_likelihood: HIGH — Niche retirement blog, small team. Editor reads emails.
prospects.widget_fit: Retirement planning often means downsizing/relocating. Affordability widget helps readers evaluate their next home.
prospects.pitch_angle: Retirement housing: 'Retirees often move to lower-cost areas — this widget helps them calculate what they can afford in their new location.'

prospects.name: Go Curry Cracker
prospects.reply_likelihood: MEDIUM-HIGH — Well-known FIRE blogger but still small team. Personal email.
prospects.widget_fit: International FIRE/travel audience. Housing decisions affect FIRE math significantly.
prospects.pitch_angle: Global FI angle: 'Whether settling down or geo-arbitraging, your readers need to know what housing budget keeps them FI.'

prospects.name: Rockstar Finance
prospects.reply_likelihood: MEDIUM — Directory/curation site. May feature free tools on resource page.
prospects.widget_fit: Resource directory that lists financial tools. Widget could be featured in tool roundup.
prospects.pitch_angle: Tool directory: 'Your resource page lists financial tools — our free widget would be a valuable addition for your readers.'

prospects.name: The Budget Mom
prospects.reply_likelihood: HIGH — Active solo blogger, engaged audience. Replies to readers.
prospects.widget_fit: Budget-focused moms making housing decisions. Affordability widget fits her budgeting tools theme.
prospects.pitch_angle: Family finance: 'Your readers budget carefully — this widget shows them exactly what home price fits their family budget.'

prospects.name: 20s Finances
prospects.reply_likelihood: HIGH — Niche blog targeting 20-somethings. Smaller site, likely owner-operated.
prospects.widget_fit: 20-somethings buying first homes. Affordability is their #1 question.
prospects.pitch_angle: First-time buyer: 'Your readers are entering the housing market for the first time — this widget answers their #1 question: how much can I afford?'

prospects.name: Debt Free Forties
prospects.reply_likelihood: HIGH — Solo blogger, debt-free focus. Very personal, owner-reads-email.
prospects.widget_fit: Debt-free audience making housing decisions. Affordability = avoiding overborrowing.
prospects.pitch_angle: Debt-free housing: 'Your audience wants to avoid mortgage traps — this widget shows them exactly what they can afford without stretching.'

selected_for_send: Retire by 40 (HIGHPRI — FI audience, mortgage widget directly relevant, high reply likelihood)
Frugalwoods (HIGHPRI — Frugality + housing fit, high reply likelihood)
My Money Wizard (HIGHPRI — Millennial homebuyer audience, high reply likelihood)
20s Finances (HIGHPRI — First-time buyer niche, high reply likelihood)
The Budget Mom (HIGHPRI — Family finance, widget fits budgeting theme)
held: Can I Retire Yet — Good fit, hold for next batch (5-send limit)
Go Curry Cracker — Good fit, hold for next batch
Budget Simple Life — Lower authority, hold
Rockstar Finance — Directory site, lower conversion probability
Debt Free Forties — Very small blog, hold
